name: cable decline press
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to a decline position.
Sit on the decline bench facing the cable machine.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position them at chest level.
Keep your feet flat on the ground and your back firmly against the bench.
Exhale and push the handles away from your body, extending your arms fully.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, squeezing your chest muscles.
Inhale and slowly return the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Adjust the cable machine to a decline position. Sit on the decline bench facing the cable machine. Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position them at chest level. Keep your feet flat on the ground and your back firmly against the bench. Exhale and push the handles away from your body, extending your arms fully. Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, squeezing your chest muscles. Inhale and slowly return the handles back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
